event_blocks.summary: In a grand green hall, Tom, a blue cat, dismissively gestures before fleeing through a large green door, prompting the New Mouse Character, a small mouse with a pink horn, to exclaim, "Oh, this is terrible, terrible!" and encourage Tom to confront a wizard, while Jerry, a brown mouse, notes Tom's unexpected bravery. Tom then crashes through a window with a loud shattering sound, startling the New Mouse Character. Dorothy, the Tin Man, and the Cowardly Lion, whimpering in fear, panic and flee into a dark, eerie forest, passing a weathered sign warning of the 'HAUNTED FOREST WITCHES CASTLE 1 MILE' and advising to 'TURN BACK'. Tom, now armed with a baseball bat, Jerry with a mallet, and the New Mouse Character cautiously approach the sign before venturing deeper into the forest. Meanwhile, the Wicked Witch of the West, observing through a crystal ball in her castle, laughs and declares, "At last! I've got that girl right where I want her! And Tom and Jerry too!" A winged demonic cat appears, and the Witch commands it to take its army to the Haunted Forest to retrieve Dorothy, her dog, and specifically Tom and Jerry. Ominous music and the sound of flying creatures accompany the scene as a swarm of cackling, winged demonic cats pursues Dorothy, the Tin Man, and the Cowardly Lion through the haunted forest. Dorothy is snatched by a cat and flown away, while Tom and Jerry run from the chaos. The New Mouse Character finds the fallen Scarecrow, who explains his dismemberment. The New Mouse Character then declares they must follow the captors, leaping onto the back of a demonic cat carrying Dorothy, biting its tail, and causing it to shriek. The cat swats her away, but she clings on, determined to rescue Dorothy amidst swirling clouds and frantic music. A winged demonic cat, clutching a struggling Tom, plummets through swirling clouds, pursued by Jerry riding a winged creature who ensnares the cat with its tail. During the aerial tussle, Tom briefly grabs a screaming Dorothy before falling toward an Eerie Pond, where a monstrous Green Crocodile snaps at him. Tom, now blue and wingless, climbs a rope with Jerry on his back to a Stone Parapet. In a dimly lit Opulent Chamber, the green-skinned Wicked Witch of the West, beside a crystal ball, mockingly comments on a dog and gestures menacingly at a frightened Dorothy, who calls for Toto. The Witch, in a sinister, cackling voice, taunts Dorothy in a chamber with red drapes and stone pillars, demanding the ruby slippers and threatening Toto. When Dorothy pleads to give up the slippers, the Witch, frustrated by their permanence, plots how to remove them delicately. Tom, dazed, is carried by a Demonic Cat with Wings while Jerry scurries around a woven basket it holds. The Witch raises her broomstick, causing Dorothy to clutch her chest, and shouts, "Run, Toto, run!" mistaking Jerry for Toto, as the Demonic Cat commands, "Catch him, you fool!" The Witch summons a dark force, declaring, "It's how long you've got to live. And it isn't long, my dear," as she and the Demonic Cat cackle. Jerry, beside a golden hourglass, is told by the Wicked Witch of the West to "play ball," shrinks with a squeak, and falls into the hourglass as sand falls. Tom, now tiny, is chased by Jerry riding on his back across rocky terrain under a stormy sky. Tom catches Jerry mid-air with a triumphant meow. Dorothy, in a stone-walled room, exclaims, "Tom! Jerry!" as the Witch confronts her, declaring, "That's the last of them. I'll come back for you, just as soon as those sands run out," before vanishing. On a castle battlement, a Dog-like Guard consults a 'Winkie Guard Handbook' with a Bear-like Guard, chanting "Yo-ho-ho!" Tom and Jerry, still tiny, crash through the battlement, knocking the guards over. The Dog-like Guard, shocked, tries to continue reading the handbook as Tom lands dazed and Jerry perches on his back. The Dog-like Guard raises his spear in alarm. On the stone battlement, the Dog-like Guard reads aloud "Rule two: Winkie Guards receive one personal day every 300 years," and "Rule three: Guards must keep all water away from the wicked witch. It's her one weakness." Tom, in a guard uniform, crashes through the battlement with Jerry on his back. The guards exclaim, "Oh, I think we have a couple of escaped prisoners!" and "You're having a busy first day, new guard. I salute you." Jerry leaps onto the Dog-like Guard's head, causing him to stumble. The guards shout, "It's them! Oh dear, seize them!" Tom collapses, and Jerry runs off. Tom tumbles down a dark, spiraling Stone Staircase with Jerry, landing in a Stone-Walled Room. A Dog-like Guard appears, holding a 'Winkie Guard Handbook', and declares, "You know, I'm just a guard, but I take my responsibilities very seriously." Tom yelps, drops Jerry, and flees through a Storage Door into a cluttered Storage Room. The guard reappears, and Tom, dazed, confronts him as the guard speaks about doing his best. Tom yelps again as the guard tells him to come along quietly.

New Mouse Character.description: A small mouse wearing a purple dress with a pink horn, initially angry and gesturing, later running through the forest with Tom and Jerry, speaking with a high-pitched, urgent voice.

Haunted Forest Sign.description: A weathered wooden sign with white text reading 'HAUNTED FOREST WITCHES CASTLE 1 MILE' and 'I'D TURN BACK IF I WERE YOU!', mounted on a gnarled tree in a dark forest.

Cowardly Lion.description: A large, brown lion with a green collar, running alongside Dorothy and the Tin Man, screaming in terror.

Demonic Cats.description: Two gray cats with wings, red capes, and black hats; one appears beside the witch, reacting with glee and cackling.

Demonic Cat with Wings.description: A gray, winged cat with a red cape and black hat, initially attacking Tom and Dorothy (as per visual context, though only carrying Tom is shown in this segment), later perched atop a stone ledge, holding a woven basket, and cackling beside the witch. It growls and hisses, and audibly shouts "Catch him, you fool!" with an aggressive voice.

Green Hall.description: A grand, emerald-green hall with ornate architecture, featuring tall columns, polished floors, and a large green door.

Crystal Ball.description: A glowing orb on a golden stand, visible on the table beside the witch, reflecting Tom and Jerry’s earlier struggle.

Tom.description: A blue cat with white paws and belly, initially tumbling down a spiral staircase while holding Jerry, accompanied by tumbling sounds and chase music. He lands triumphantly with a 'boing' sound, but yelps in surprise when the guard appears, dropping Jerry. He then crashes through a wooden door with a loud 'crash' into a storage room, dazed, and yelps again during the confrontation.

Dorothy.description: A young girl with brown hair tied with blue bows, wearing a blue dress and white apron, initially looking alarmed as the witch confronts her, exclaiming 'Tom! Jerry!', then showing determination after the witch vanishes.

Wicked Witch of the West.description: A green-skinned witch with a black pointed hat and cloak, holding a broomstick, casting a spell that shrinks Jerry while cackling and speaking menacingly, saying 'play ball, Jerry!' and later confronting Dorothy with the line, 'That's the last of them. I'll come back for you, just as soon as those sands run out,' before vanishing with a whoosh.

Tin Man.description: A metallic figure with a silver body and green vest, holding a small axe, running in alarm and screaming in terror.

Jerry.description: A small brown mouse with large ears, initially held by Tom, then dropped when Tom yelps in surprise. He scurries away, accompanied by frantic chase music and cartoon sounds, later seen running past Tom and the guard in the storage room.

Scarecrow.description: A straw-filled figure with a brown hat and green shirt, standing beside Dorothy, arms outstretched in surprise, and later looking up at the sky with concern, speaking with a high-pitched, frantic voice about his dismembered parts.

Green Crocodile.description: A large, green reptile with sharp teeth and yellow eyes, it erupts from the eerie pond with a growl and roar.

Opulent Chamber.description: A dimly lit, grand room with red drapes, golden tables, and stone pillars, where the witch and demonic cat plot, creating a sinister atmosphere enhanced by the suspenseful background music.

Eerie Pond.description: A dark, still body of water surrounded by twisted trees, from which a green crocodile emerges with sharp teeth, snapping at Tom.

Stone Parapet.description: A flat stone ledge atop a structure, where Tom and Jerry reach safety, overlooking a dark, stormy landscape.

Dark Stone Wall.description: A tall, dark stone wall with a rope attached, against which Tom and Jerry climb, showing a stormy sky above.

Broomstick.description: A wooden broomstick with a straw bristle end, held by the witch, used to conjure magic, leaving a swirling effect as she prepares to cast a spell, accompanied by magical blast and whoosh sounds.

Dog-like Guard.description: A white dog in blue armor with red trim and a tall black hat, holding a red spear and consulting a 'Winkie Guard Handbook'. He speaks in a deep, formal, slightly pompous voice, stating he takes his responsibilities 'very seriously' and that a good guard 'always does the very best he can'. He reappears stoically as Tom crashes into the storage room, and begins to speak about coming along 'quietly'.

Winkie Guard Handbook.description: A red book with gold trim and a title 'Winkie Guard Handbook' featuring crossed spears, held by the dog-like guard as he speaks formally about his duties.

Bear-like Guard.description: A bear-like character in blue armor with red trim and a tall black hat, holding a red spear, initially observing the dog-like guard, then reacting with shock and alarm as Tom and Jerry crash through the battlement.

Storage Door.description: A wooden door with the word 'STORAGE' written in white capital letters, which Tom crashes through with a loud 'crash' sound, leading into a cluttered room filled with barrels and crates.

Stone Staircase.description: A dark, spiraling stone staircase with torches on the walls, through which Tom tumbles at the beginning of the segment.

Storage Room.description: A cluttered, dimly lit room with stone walls, cobblestone floors, wooden barrels, and stacked crates, where Tom crashes after fleeing the guard.

Stone-Walled Room.description: A dimly lit room with stone walls, a wooden chair, and a torch, where Tom stands holding Jerry before the guard appears.

Jerry, riding atop a large, dark, winged creature, swings its tail like a lasso, ensnaring the demonic cat. The scene opens in a dimly lit, opulent chamber with red drapes, stone pillars, and golden tables, establishing a sinister atmosphere underscored by suspenseful background music. The Wicked Witch of the West, green-skinned and clad in a black pointed hat and cloak, confronts Dorothy, who wears her iconic blue dress and red shoes. The Witch, speaking with a sinister, cackling voice, taunts Dorothy, saying, "It's so kind of you to visit me in my loneliness," before menacingly demanding, "Now, give me those ruby slippers, or your dog goes into the river!" Dorothy, her voice trembling with fear, pleads, "No, please! Take the slippers!" The Witch, with a manipulative tone, responds, "What a good little girl. I knew you'd listen to reason," but then, with a magical blast, she exclaims in frustration, "I should have remembered! Those slippers will never come off as long as you're alive!" She then plots aloud, "That's not what's worrying me. It's how to do it. These things must be done delicately, or you hurt the spell." Meanwhile, Tom, the blue cat, is shown dazed on the floor, then being carried by a growling and hissing Demonic Cat with Wings. Jerry, the brown mouse, appears determined, scurrying around a woven basket held by the Demonic Cat. The Witch, with a sinister grin, raises her broomstick, from which a swirling magical effect emanates, causing Dorothy to clutch her chest in terror. The Witch shouts, "Run, Toto, run!" (likely mistaking Jerry for Toto), while the Demonic Cat, with an aggressive voice, commands, "Catch him, you fool!" Dorothy screams in terror as Tom lets out a distinct meow, both caught in peril. The Witch’s magic intensifies as she raises her hand, summoning a dark, swirling force, and menacingly declares, "Do you see that? It's how long you've got to live. And it isn't long, my dear," as she and the Demonic Cat cackle in triumph, leaving Tom and Jerry vulnerable to the impending magical confrontation.

The segment opens with Jerry the brown mouse, standing beside a golden hourglass filled with red sand, as the Wicked Witch of the West, a green-skinned witch in a black pointed hat and cloak, appears with a broomstick, cackling menacingly and telling Jerry, "play ball, Jerry!" She casts a spell with a magical sound, causing Jerry to shrink with a squeak and fall into the hourglass, accompanied by the sound of falling sand and the Witch's cackle. The scene shifts to a stormy, mountainous landscape, filled with the sound of wind and thunder, where Tom the blue cat, now tiny, is being chased by Jerry, who rides on his back as Tom frantically meows and leaps across rocky terrain. Tom’s expression shifts from panic to a mischievous grin, and he lets out a triumphant meow as he catches Jerry mid-air. A whoosh and crash sound transition to Dorothy, a young girl with brown hair and a blue dress, standing in a stone-walled room with red drapes, looking alarmed as she exclaims, "Tom! Jerry!" The Witch confronts her, pointing menacingly, and declares, "That's the last of them. I'll come back for you, just as soon as those sands run out," followed by another cackle. The Witch then vanishes with a whoosh, leaving Dorothy alone, her expression shifting from fear to determination. The setting changes to a castle battlement under a dark sky, where two guards in blue armor with red trim and tall black hats stand watch. One guard, the Dog-like Guard, consults a red book titled 'Winkie Guard Handbook' while the other, the Bear-like Guard, looks on. They chant "Yo-ho-ho!" before the Dog-like Guard authoritatively states, "And halt. Excellent work, new guard. Any questions so far?" The Bear-like Guard, with a questioning voice, asks, "Yeah, what does that yo-ho-ho song mean?" to which the Dog-like Guard replies, "I have absolutely no idea. Let's review what we learned today. Rule one!" Suddenly, with a loud crash, Tom and Jerry, still tiny, crash through the battlement, knocking the guards over. The Dog-like Guard, now holding the handbook, looks shocked as he tries to continue, "Winky guards must keep track of their hours and submit..." before being cut off by the chaos, with Tom landing dazed and Jerry perched triumphantly on his back. The Dog-like Guard then raises his spear in alarm, while the Bear-like Guard remains stunned, as the chaotic chase continues.

The scene opens on a stone battlement under a stormy, dark blue sky, where a white Dog-like Guard in blue armor with red trim and a tall black hat, holding a red spear, consults a red 'Winkie Guard Handbook'. He speaks in a clear male voice, instructing a "new guard" about procedures, asking "Something wrong, new guard?" He then reads aloud "Rule two: Winkie Guards receive one personal day every 300 years," and "Rule three: Guards must keep all water away from the wicked witch. It's her one weakness." A Bear-like Guard, similarly armored, looks on with a weary expression. Suddenly, with a loud crash and whoosh, Tom the blue cat, now in a similar guard uniform, crashes through the battlement, accompanied by a "boing" sound. Jerry the brown mouse is perched triumphantly on Tom’s back. The guards react in shock; the Dog-like Guard drops his book, exclaiming "Oh, I think we have a couple of escaped prisoners!" and then, addressing Tom, "You're having a busy first day, new guard. I salute you." The Bear-like Guard recoils. Jerry then leaps off Tom’s back with a "boing" and lands on the Dog-like Guard’s head with a "thud," causing him to stumble. The Bear-like Guard, now alarmed, raises his spear in defense. Tom, dazed, stumbles forward, while the Dog-like Guard exclaims, "It's them! Oh dear, seize them!" Jerry scurries away. The flustered Dog-like Guard tries to regain composure, while the Bear-like Guard looks on in disbelief. The scene ends with Tom collapsing to the ground with a "thud" and "clatter," his guard uniform disheveled, and Jerry running off into the shadows, leaving the guards stunned and bewildered. The Dog-like Guard's voice concludes, "I'm afraid this won't look good on your personal evaluation."

Jerry scurries out from under Tom’s feet as the dog-like guard reappears, still stoic and holding his spear. Tom, dazed, stands up and confronts the guard, who continues his monologue, stating, "It's not a glamorous job, but a good guard always does the very best he can." Tom yelps again in response, and the guard begins to speak about coming along "quietly," as the frantic chase music and cartoon sound effects intensify, maintaining the tense, medieval, dungeon-like atmosphere.

event_blocks.summary: In a dimly lit, stone-walled storage room, Tom the blue cat nervously held Jerry the brown mouse, with a voice mentioning "an extra ration of gruel." Startled by the voice, Tom dropped Jerry, who scurried away with a distinct sound. A dog-like guard in blue armor, consulting the 'Winkie Guard Handbook', raised his red spear as Tom attempted to flee. A voice exclaimed "Gotcha!" and another commanded, "Arrest them, old new guard!" Suddenly, a bear-like guard in similar armor burst through a wooden 'STORAGE' door with a crash, charging at Tom with his spear. Tom dodged, causing the bear-like guard to stumble and drop his spear with a clatter. Tom seized the spear and swung it wildly, knocking the bear-like guard down. The dog-like guard, now shocked, watched as Tom, still holding the spear, turned to face Jerry, who was perched on the dog-like guard’s head. A voice, possibly the dog-like guard, questioned, "Are you sure you completed basic training?" Tom lunged at Jerry, but the dazed dog-like guard fell backward, his spear clattering to the floor. Tom, disoriented, crashed into a stone wall, dislodging a torch that ignited the wall. The scene shifted to a golden-lit stone corridor where a dazed Tom lay on the floor, approached by a triumphant Jerry. The dog-like guard, now accompanied by two other guards, stood over the defeated Tom. Jerry, adorned with a pink horn and a black cape, appeared beside Tom, gesturing dramatically. The scene then cut to a grand, opulent chamber where voices exclaimed, "Jerry, it's us! Boy, are we glad to see you!" and explained, "Toto found us and let us back here," before asking, "Where's Dorothy?" A witch-like figure with green skin and a black pointed hat confronted Dorothy, who watched in fear as the witch raised her broomstick. A voice exclaimed "Witch! Zippers! Ouch!" and revealed, "She's locked in the tower with only moments left to live." The witch vanished in a puff of smoke with magical whooshing sounds, leaving Dorothy alone and determined. The final shot depicted Dorothy standing beside a large golden table with a crystal ball, her red ruby slippers visible, as she looked up at the sky with resolve, prompting a voice to exclaim "Oh, my!" and call out "Dorothy!"

Storage Door.description: A wooden door with the word 'STORAGE' written in white capital letters, which the bear-like guard bursts through with a loud crash.

New Mouse Character.description: A small mouse wearing a purple dress with a pink horn, initially angry and gesturing, later running through the forest with Tom and Jerry, and heard speaking lines like 'Jerry, it's us!', 'Boy, are we glad to see you!', 'Toto found us and let us back here.', 'Where's Dorothy?', 'Witch! Zippers! Ouch!', 'She's locked in the tower with only moments left to live', 'Oh, my!', and calling out 'Dorothy!'.

Opulent Chamber.description: A dimly lit, grand room with red drapes, golden tables, and stone pillars, where the witch confronts Dorothy, and where voices are heard discussing Dorothy's whereabouts and predicament.

Golden Table.description: A large, ornate golden table with intricate carvings and a crystal ball on top, located in the opulent chamber, where Dorothy stands after the witch vanishes.

Wicked Witch of the West.description: A green-skinned witch with a black pointed hat and cloak, holding a broomstick, confronting Dorothy with a sinister grin, raising her broomstick, and then vanishing in a puff of smoke with magical whooshing sounds.

Jerry.description: A small brown mouse with large ears, initially held by Tom, then dropped and scurrying away with a distinct sound, later perched on the dog-like guard’s head, and finally standing triumphantly beside Tom, and later heard being addressed by others.

Dog-like Guard.description: A white dog in blue armor with red trim and a tall black hat, holding a red spear, initially consulting a red book titled 'Winkie Guard Handbook', then reacting with shock, possibly speaking about 'basic training' to the bear-like guard, and finally falling backward dazed, also heard giving the command 'Arrest them, old new guard!'.

Winkie Guard Handbook.description: A red book with gold trim and a title 'Winkie Guard Handbook' featuring crossed spears, held by the dog-like guard, which he consults before dropping in surprise.

Storage Room.description: A cluttered, dimly lit room with stone walls, cobblestone floors, wooden barrels, and stacked crates, where Tom crashes after fleeing the guard, filled with sounds of scuffles and impacts.

Red Ruby Slippers.description: Dorothy’s iconic red shoes, visible on her feet, standing near Tom, Jerry, and the New Mouse Character, and later seen as Tom cowers beside them.

Dorothy.description: A young girl with brown hair tied with blue bows, wearing a blue dress and white apron, initially looking alarmed as the witch confronts her, then showing determination after the witch vanishes, and later heard being called by name.

Stone-Walled Room.description: A dimly lit room with stone walls, a wooden chair, and a torch, where Tom stands holding Jerry before the guard appears, filled with sounds of scuffles and impacts.

Bear-like Guard.description: A bear-like character in blue armor with red trim and a tall black hat, holding a red spear, initially bursting through a wooden door marked 'STORAGE' with a loud crash, charging at Tom, and then falling to the ground after being knocked by Tom’s spear, possibly exclaiming 'Gotcha!' during the chase.

Crystal Ball.description: A glowing orb on a golden stand, visible on the table beside the witch, reflecting Tom and Jerry’s earlier struggle.

Tom.description: A blue cat with white paws and belly, initially holding Jerry, then dropping him with a start, fleeing from guards, grabbing a spear, swinging it wildly, and finally lying dazed and defeated on the floor amidst sounds of struggle and impacts.

event_blocks.summary: Within a dimly lit, stone-walled chamber, Dorothy, clad in her blue dress and apron, sat by a glowing purple crystal ball, anxiously inquiring, "Where are you?" and exclaiming, "It's him!" as a young man appeared in the ball. She pleaded, "Oh, him, don't go away! I'm frightened! Come back! Come back!" The image shifted to the Wicked Witch of the West, who cackled menacingly, "I'll give you, him, my pretty!" causing Dorothy to react with shock. A whooshing sound accompanied this magical shift. The scene then transitioned to a dark storage room where Tom, the blue cat, was pursued by two armored guards, their armor clanking amidst animal sounds. A dog-like guard, holding a red spear, shouted, "Toto will find Dorothy, come on!" Tom, startled, dropped Jerry the brown mouse, who scurried away. Tom grabbed a red spear and stumbled, only to be knocked down by a bear-like guard bursting through a door marked 'STORAGE'. Jerry, finding a small green bottle, used it with a "splat" and "poof" to shrink Tom into a puddle of blue liquid. A new gray mouse character with a pink horn and purple dress confronted Jerry, questioning, "You mean that water is the witch's one weakness?" Jerry, holding the bottle, declared, "I'll take that!" but the new mouse grabbed it and ran off into the shadows, leaving Jerry surprised. Later, in a dimly lit courtyard enclosed by a black iron fence, Tom approached a stone well with a wooden cover, pulling a bucket on a rope. As he lowered it, Jerry climbed the rope and rode it down. Tom pulled the rope back up, but Jerry swung out and escaped with a "whoosh." Frustrated, Tom yanked the rope hard, splashing water everywhere and causing him to slip and fall backward onto the stone ground with a "thud," appearing dazed and wet, while Jerry stood triumphantly on the well's edge.

His voice is heard saying 'Toto will find Dorothy, come on!'

Her voice is heard questioning, 'You mean that water is the witch's one weakness?'

His voice is heard asking 'There's more?', then declaring 'I'll take that!', and later giving instructions, 'You and Tom find some more water. I'll...' before being cut off.

Her voice is heard anxiously asking 'Where are you?' and exclaiming 'It's him!', then pleading 'Oh, him, don't go away! I'm frightened! Come back! Come back!'

Bear-like Guard.description: A bear-like character in blue armor with red trim and a tall black hat, holding a red spear, initially bursting through a wooden door marked 'STORAGE', charging at Tom, and then falling to the ground after being knocked by Tom’s spear.

His actions are accompanied by cat hissing/growling sounds, and a distinct 'splat' and 'poof' when he is shrunk.

Blue Cat.description: A blue cat with white paws and belly, initially smiling mischievously as he lowers a bucket into the well, accompanied by pulley creaks and splashes, then becoming frustrated and angry, yanking the rope with more splashes and creaks, and finally falling backward with a distinct 'thud' and dazed surprise after pulling the rope too hard.

Black Iron Fence.description: A tall, ornate black iron fence with pointed tips, enclosing the courtyard around the well, with a gate visible in the background and a stormy sky visible beyond it.

raw_segments: The segment opens in a dimly lit, stone-walled chamber with a mystical atmosphere, where Dorothy, dressed in her signature blue dress and white apron with a blue bow in her hair, sits beside a glowing purple crystal ball on an ornate golden stand. Dorothy's voice is heard, anxiously asking "Where are you?" and exclaiming "It's him!" as a concerned young man with blonde hair appears in the crystal ball. She pleads, "Oh, him, don't go away! I'm frightened! Come back! Come back!" As she leans closer, the image shifts to reveal the green-skinned Wicked Witch of the West, who cackles menacingly, declaring, "I'll give you, him, my pretty!" before erupting in maniacal laughter, causing Dorothy to react with shock and fear, covering her mouth. A whooshing sound accompanies this magical shift. The scene then cuts to a dark, stone-walled storage room where Tom, the blue cat, is being chased by two armored guards amidst clanking armor and animal sounds, including Tom's own hissing/growling. A dog-like guard, holding a red spear, is heard saying "Toto will find Dorothy, come on!" as he pursues Tom. Tom, holding Jerry the brown mouse, is startled and drops Jerry, who scurries away. Tom grabs a red spear and stumbles forward, only to be knocked down by the bear-like guard bursting through a door marked 'STORAGE'. Jerry, now free, finds a small green bottle and, with a cartoon "splat" and "poof," uses it to shrink Tom, who collapses into a puddle of blue liquid. Jerry's voice is heard asking "There's more?" before he encounters a new mouse character, a gray mouse with a pink horn and purple dress, who appears angry and confronts Jerry. The new mouse's voice is heard questioning, "You mean that water is the witch's one weakness?" Jerry, holding the bottle, tries to reason, then declares "I'll take that!" as he grabs the bottle. The new mouse then grabs the bottle and runs off, leaving Jerry looking surprised and standing alone, holding the bottle, as the new mouse runs away into the shadows, with Jerry's voice cut off mid-sentence saying, "You and Tom find some more water. I'll..."

The segment opens in a dimly lit, moonlit courtyard enclosed by a black iron fence, focusing on a stone well with a wooden cover and pulley system, as dramatic orchestral music plays, punctuated by distant, seemingly unrelated speech about "saving Dorothy" and "good luck, fellas." The Blue Cat, Tom, approaches with a mischievous grin, pulling a wooden bucket on a rope, the pulley system creaking. He lowers the bucket into the well, and a splash is heard as Jerry, the Brown Mouse, climbs onto the rope and rides it down into the water. Tom, still smiling, pulls the rope back up with a creak, but Jerry cleverly swings out of the bucket with a "whoosh" and escapes, followed by a "boing" sound. Tom, now frustrated, yanks the rope again, accompanied by more splashing and creaking, only for Jerry to reappear perched atop the rising bucket. In a fit of rage, Tom grabs the rope and yanks it hard, causing the bucket to tip and splash water everywhere with a loud "thud" and "splash." He slips and falls backward, landing on the stone ground with a distinct "thud," his expression shifting from anger to dazed surprise, as playful, slightly triumphant music begins. The scene concludes with Tom lying dazed and wet, while Jerry stands triumphantly on the well's edge, looking down with a smug expression, accompanied by a final "boing" sound, all within the dark, moody environment under a stormy sky.

The well is the source of loud splashes, gurgling, and sounds of struggle during the crocodile's attack.

Green Bottle.description: A small green bottle held by the New Mouse Character, which she uses to shrink Tom and later clutches after escaping the hourglass.

Dorothy.description: A young girl with brown hair tied with blue bows, wearing a blue dress and white apron, standing with her companions on a stone parapet, looking alarmed as the witch conjures magic, exclaiming 'Now we're up!' in a clear voice.

Tom.description: A blue cat with white paws and belly, initially holding a wooden bucket, then dropping it, being attacked by a green crocodile, and finally collapsing dazed. He vocalizes with a distinct 'Ow!' when attacked, a surprised 'What?!', and a later 'Hmm, aha!' of realization, followed by a confused sound.

Stone Parapet.description: A flat stone ledge atop a structure, where the group peers over the edge into a stormy landscape, and where the Blue Cat and New Mouse Character are perched.
