Ring around the rosy, a pocket full of spears. Thought you were pretty foxy, didn't you?', 'Silly.', and 'How about a little fire, Scarecrow?'. Her cackles are frequent and loud.

Fishing Rod.description: A long fishing rod with a blue reel, used by Jerry to trap Tom, with a line attached to the bucket.

Woven Basket.description: A brown woven basket with a handle, held by the demonic cat, which Jerry scurries around and interacts with during the confrontation.

Woman in Green Dress.description: A woman with brown hair, wearing a green dress and white apron, holding a basket, standing beside the group in the corridor, looking alarmed and exclaiming 'Oh my!'

Green Crocodile.description: A large, green reptile with sharp teeth and yellow eyes, erupting from a well to attack Tom, then dragging the bucket and Tom across the stone floor.

Rope.description: A long, beige rope used by the New Mouse Character to lasso the Blue Cat and swing across a gap on the rooftop, later becoming electrified with zapping sounds and pulling the Blue Cat violently.

Man in Green Hat.description: A man with a green hat, wearing a green vest and brown pants, holding a shovel, looking up in alarm and directing 'That way!'

Broomstick.description: A wooden broomstick with a straw bristle end, held by the witch, used to conjure magic, leaving a swirling effect as she prepares to cast a spell, accompanied by magical whoosh and explosion sounds.

Stormy Sky.description: A dark, cloudy sky with lightning and rain, dominating the rooftop and mountainous landscape, intensifying as the witch’s magic causes lightning to strike the cloud, accompanied by thunder and wind sounds.

raw_segments: The segment opens with the creaking of a rope as the Blue Cat, with a mischievous chuckle, lowers the Brown Mouse in a wooden bucket into a stone well under a stormy night sky. A splash is heard as the bucket plunges, leaving the Blue Cat to let out a frustrated meow as the rope goes slack. The scene shifts to a dimly lit stone-walled room where the Blue Cat, now holding Jerry, is confronted by a dog-like guard in blue armor. The guard growls, rustles the pages of his 'Winkie Guard Handbook', then charges, prompting the Blue Cat to yelp and flee down a stone staircase with tumbling sounds. He crashes into a storage room as a bear-like guard bursts through a 'STORAGE' door with a loud roar and crash, also charging. The Blue Cat grabs a red spear, grunting as he fights back and knocks the bear-like guard to the ground with clashing sounds. The scene cuts to a golden hourglass with red sand, where the New Mouse Character, in a purple dress with a pink horn, is trapped. A companion's voice (the Cowardly Lion) calls "Dorothy, are you in there?", to which the New Mouse Character urgently responds "I'm here! Oh, please hurry! The witch will be back when the hourglass is empty!". Dorothy, in her blue dress and red slippers, rushes to the hourglass, horrified, letting out a gasp. The Witch-Like Woman, in a green dress and pointed hat, appears with the Cowardly Lion, who whimpers nervously, and the Tin Man, who clanks metallically. The Witch-Like Woman cackles as they use a mallet to smash the hourglass with a distinct breaking sound, freeing the New Mouse Character, who lets out a relieved squeak. The Witch-Like Woman then vanishes, leaving the group in shock. The Cowardly Lion exclaims "Oh, Dorothy, we found you!", and the Tin Man says "Oh!". The New Mouse Character runs off with scampering sounds, clutching a green bottle, and says "Oh, thank you!".

The segment opens with urgent scurrying sounds as a small New Mouse Character, wearing a purple dress with a pink horn, scurries between Dorothy’s red ruby slippers, the Cowardly Lion’s paws, and the Tin Man’s feet, declaring, "Oh, come on! There's no time to lose!" The scene shifts to a dark, stone-walled dungeon where green-skinned, armored guards with red capes and ornate spears march in formation to the clanking of their armor. The New Mouse Character appears before them, confronting the guards with a defiant stance. The camera cuts to a wide shot of Dorothy, the Scarecrow, the Tin Man, and the Cowardly Lion, all looking alarmed in a dimly lit stone corridor, frantically shouting "Run!" as the Cowardly Lion lets out a frightened "Oh!". The New Mouse Character runs toward them, leading them through a narrow passage with frantic footsteps. The group emerges onto a stone parapet overlooking a stormy, mountainous landscape, peering over the edge in fear, with the Cowardly Lion whining, "We're trapped, don't you know?" The New Mouse Character then runs ahead, confidently stating, "I'll handle this!" as she leads the group through a large red banner with a black, stylized face design, which she climbs atop. She leaps from the banner, causing the guards to scatter in panic with shouts and clanking, as she lands triumphantly. One Green-Skinned Guard booms, "Who dares disturb my cookie time?!" before the guards collapse, dropping their spears. The New Mouse Character triumphantly declares, "That's right! That's what you get when you mess with a big Munchkin!" as the group rushes forward, with the Cowardly Lion exclaiming, "Very impressive! Now run!" The scene transitions to a dimly lit stone corridor where Tom the blue cat, holding a wooden bucket, is startled by Jerry the brown mouse, who scurries past with a squeak. Tom drops the bucket with a yelp, spilling water. The New Mouse Character appears, confronting Tom with a determined expression. The final shot shows Tom, dazed and covered in water, as the New Mouse Character stands triumphantly beside him, having outwitted the guards and the cat, ending with her triumphant squeak.

The segment opens with a small mouse in a purple dress with a pink horn scurrying across a stone floor, as a male voice urgently exclaims, "Nice doing! We need that water fast!" This is followed by a blue cat, Tom, holding a brown mouse, Jerry, in a dimly lit stone-walled room. Startled, Tom drops Jerry, who scurries away as Tom flees down a stone staircase. The scene shifts to a dark, stormy courtyard where Tom, holding a wooden bucket, climbs a stone wall to draw water from a well. As he lowers the bucket, a green crocodile erupts from the water with loud splashes and gurgles, snapping at Tom, who yelps and struggles. Tom is pulled back up, but the crocodile follows, dragging the bucket and Tom into the well amidst more frantic splashing and Tom's distressed cries. A triumphant "Hoo-ah!" and "Yeah!" are heard as the crocodile emerges on the stone floor, dragging the bucket, which Jerry then uses to trap Tom. Jerry, now holding a fishing rod, reels Tom in, eliciting more yelps and struggles from Tom, who briefly escapes only to be caught again by the crocodile. The crocodile, still on the stone floor, is pulled by Jerry’s fishing line, dragging the struggling Tom along. Tom, dazed, walks away with the bucket, while Jerry scurries off. The scene transitions to a stone corridor where Dorothy, the Scarecrow, the Tin Man, the Cowardly Lion, and a woman in a green dress (Glinda) rush down the stairs, alarmed, exclaiming "Whoa!" and "We're safe!" They are confronted by a green-skinned Wicked Witch of the West and a demonic cat with wings, who cackle menacingly. The Witch, holding a broomstick, conjures magic, and the group reacts in fear. The Witch's menacing voice then asks, "Going so soon? Why, our little party's just beginning!" before she vanishes, leaving the group stunned, as Tom, holding the bucket, walks past them, unaware of the danger, accompanied by ominous, building music throughout the witch's appearance.

The segment opens in a dimly lit stone corridor with torches, where the Wicked Witch of the West, a green-skinned witch in a black pointed hat and cloak, cackles menacingly from a ledge above, accompanied by a demonic cat with wings and a red cape, whose cackles are heard alongside hers. A male voice, likely the Scarecrow, exclaims, "We're trapped, trapped like mice, rats!" Below, a group of green-skinned guards in blue armor with red capes and tall black hats stand in formation, holding spears. Dorothy, the Scarecrow, the Tin Man, and the Cowardly Lion descend a stone staircase, looking alarmed amidst sounds of chaos and clanging. The Witch, speaking with a menacing, cackling voice, instructs, "Don't hurt them right away. Let them think about it a little first." She then holds a large golden hourglass with red sand, smashing it with a mallet, causing an explosion and screams. As the group flees and the guards charge, the Witch frantically commands, "Seize them! Seize them! Stop them, you fools! Seize them!" The scene shifts to a stone parapet overlooking a stormy landscape. Tom, the blue cat, is seen holding a wooden bucket, which he drops, spilling water with a splash. He lets out an "Ow!" as a green crocodile emerges from a well, dragging him and the bucket across the stone floor. Tom exclaims, "What?!" echoed by Jerry, the brown mouse, who scurries around with a new mouse character in a purple dress with a pink horn. The new mouse character, with a firm, high-pitched voice, admonishes, "Stop fooling around! Dorothy needs that water!" Tom then has a moment of realization, "Hmm, aha!" The new mouse character uses a fishing rod to trap Tom, who collapses dazed. The Witch reappears, cackling, as the guards are scattered. The new mouse character climbs a red banner with a black face design, leaping to defeat the guards, all set to an orchestral, adventurous score. The scene ends with Tom lying dazed on the stone floor, the bucket beside him, as the new mouse character stands triumphantly.

The segment opens on a stone rooftop under a stormy sky, where the Blue Cat, a blue cat with white paws and belly, initially holding a wooden bucket, is startled by the New Mouse Character, a small mouse wearing a purple dress with a pink horn, who playfully tosses a roll of toilet paper. The Blue Cat lets out a "meow" as he drops the bucket with a "thud," spilling water, while the New Mouse Character emits a playful "squeak." The New Mouse Character then uses a long rope to lasso the Blue Cat, dragging him across the rooftop amidst the sound of heavy rain and howling wind, punctuated by a distant thunder "boom." The scene shifts to a stone corridor where Dorothy, a young girl with brown hair tied with blue bows, wearing a blue dress and white apron, exclaims "There!" as she and her companions, including the Scarecrow, a straw-filled figure who asks "What?", the Tin Man, a metallic figure who shouts "This way!", and the Cowardly Lion, a large, brown lion who urges "Come on!", along with the Woman in Green Dress, who cries "Oh my!", and the Man in Green Hat, who directs "That way!", peer anxiously over a parapet as rain falls. The Wicked Witch of the West, a green-skinned witch with a black pointed hat and cloak, appears on a nearby tower, cackling menacingly with a distinct, echoing laugh and magical sound effects. The camera pans to reveal a group of Green-Skinned Guards, armored soldiers with green skin, red capes, tall black hats, and ornate spears, marching up a stone staircase with grunts and shouts. The Blue Cat, now holding the rope and letting out a strained "meow," is seen running with the New Mouse Character clinging to it, as they navigate the rooftop. The New Mouse Character then uses the rope to swing across a gap with a whooshing sound, narrowly avoiding falling, and later emits a "squeak" after a "thwack." The Blue Cat, now holding the rope taut, looks determined as he prepares to confront the guards, while a loud thunderclap and dramatic music swell. The scene ends with the Blue Cat, letting out a determined "meow," and the New Mouse Character, with a triumphant "squeak," standing triumphantly on the rooftop, the storm raging around them, as the guards, now shouting in frustration, approach from below.

The segment opens with ominous, suspenseful music and the sound of howling wind as a wide shot reveals a stormy, mountainous landscape under a dark, cloudy sky. Perched on a stone parapet, the Blue Cat holds a long rope with the New Mouse Character clinging to his back. The scene quickly shifts to a dimly lit stone corridor where the Tin Man, Scarecrow, Cowardly Lion, and Dorothy are confronted by Green-Skinned Guards and the Wicked Witch of the West. The Witch, with her green skin and black pointed hat, cackles menacingly with a high-pitched, sinister voice, holding a broomstick and taunting, "Well, well. Ring around the rosy, a pocket full of spears. Thought you were pretty foxy, didn't you?" A magical whoosh accompanies her spell. The camera cuts to the rooftop, where the Blue Cat, with the New Mouse Character still clinging, swings the rope toward a dark cloud, which suddenly rains water into a wooden bucket below, accompanied by magical sound effects. Back in the corridor, the Witch smirks and cackles as she watches the rain. Dorothy exclaims, "Now we're up!" before the Witch dismissively adds, "Silly. The last to go, we'll see the first four go before her." Scarecrow yells, "Help!" as the Witch cackles again. The Blue Cat then uses the rope to pull the now water-filled bucket toward the edge of the parapet. In the corridor, the Witch raises her broomstick, conjuring a fiery spell that engulfs the group, causing them to recoil in fear. She cackles, "How about a little fire, Scarecrow?" to which Scarecrow screams, "Oh no! I'm burning! I'm burning!" followed by a pained gurgling sound amidst the fire effects. The Blue Cat, now drenched, swings the bucket again, while the Witch, laughing, raises her hand as lightning strikes the cloud, intensifying the storm with thunder and intense electrical zapping sounds. The Blue Cat is then shown being pulled by the now electrified rope, struggling and letting out a pained yelp/scream to maintain his grip. The Witch, still cackling, raises her broomstick again, and the scene ends with the Blue Cat being yanked violently by the rope, his body contorted as he is pulled toward the edge of the parapet, while the New Mouse Character clings desperately to his back.

event_blocks.summary: On a dark stone castle rooftop at night, a blue cat is violently yanked across the surface by an electrified rope, whirring and crackling with electricity, by a New Mouse Character, causing the cat to splash water from a bucket before crashing into a stone wall with a thud and collapsing, soaked and motionless, while the New Mouse Character scrambles off. In a dimly lit stone corridor, Dorothy, the Scarecrow, Tin Man, and Cowardly Lion face the Wicked Witch of the West and Green-Skinned Guards; the witch cackles and conjures a fiery spell, but Dorothy shouts for Jerry to use water, and despite the witch's command not to, the spell backfires with a magical explosion and green smoke, causing the witch to scream about melting and her wickedness being destroyed before vanishing in a puff of smoke and ash, leaving her broomstick behind. The Green-Skinned Guards react with shock, and the New Mouse Character, holding a green bottle, and Jerry the brown mouse, squeaking nearby, observe the stunned group and guards, one of whom declares the witch dead, prompting Dorothy's distressed denial. Torches cast flickering light on the tense, magical atmosphere.

Tin Man.description: A metallic figure with a silver body and green vest, holding a small axe, running in alarm as the witch’s spell engulfs the group and then standing stunned after the witch’s defeat.

New Mouse Character.description: A small mouse wearing a purple dress with a pink horn, clinging to the Blue Cat’s back during the rope swing, then standing beside Dorothy holding a green bottle after the witch’s defeat.

Scarecrow.description: A straw-filled figure with a brown hat and green shirt, standing beside Dorothy, arms outstretched in surprise as the witch’s magic erupts and then looking shocked after her vanishing.

Cowardly Lion.description: A large, brown lion with a green collar, running alongside Dorothy and the Tin Man, looking frightened as the witch’s magic intensifies and then appearing shocked after the witch vanishes.

Green-Skinned Guards.description: Armored soldiers with green skin, red capes, tall black hats, and ornate spears, reacting with shock as the witch vanishes, with one guard declaring in a deep voice, 'She's dead. You killed her.'

Broomstick.description: A wooden broomstick with a straw bristle end, held by the witch, used to conjure magic, left discarded on the floor after her disappearance.

Stone Corridor.description: A dimly lit, stone-walled passage with torches, where the group is confronted by the witch and her guards, and where the witch conjures her spells, now filled with smoke and ash after her defeat.

Green Bottle.description: A small green bottle held by the New Mouse Character, which she uses to shrink Tom and later clutches after escaping the hourglass, now visible in her hand after the witch’s vanishing.

Dorothy.description: A young girl with brown hair tied with blue bows, wearing a blue dress and white apron, looking alarmed during the witch’s spell, urgently crying, 'Jerry, quick! The water!', and later, with a distressed voice, stating, 'I didn't mean to kill her. Really, I didn't,' after the witch’s disappearance.

Wicked Witch of the West.description: A green-skinned witch with a black pointed hat and cloak, cackling menacingly, holding a broomstick, and conjuring fiery and lightning spells. She shouts, 'Don't throw that water!' before being engulfed in green smoke, screaming, 'You cursed brat! Look what you've done! I'm melting, melting! What a world! Who could have thought a good little girl like you would destroy my beautiful wickedness! Look out! I'm going!' Her voice distorts and fades into a gurgling sound as she vanishes.

Blue Cat.description: A blue cat (Tom) with white paws and belly, initially swinging on an electrified rope, then collapsing dazed and soaked after crashing into a wall, letting out a dazed groan.

Jerry.description: A small brown mouse with large ears, scurrying near Dorothy and the New Mouse Character after the witch’s vanishing, making quick squeaking sounds.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a dramatic nighttime scene atop a dark stone castle, where a blue cat (Tom) is violently yanked across the rooftop by an electrified rope, pulled by a small mouse in a purple dress with a pink horn (New Mouse Character). The air crackles with electricity and the rope whirs as Tom swings, splashing water from a wooden bucket before crashing into a stone wall with a loud thud and a dazed groan, collapsing in a heap, soaked. The New Mouse Character scrambles off his back as Tom lies motionless. The scene shifts to a dimly lit stone corridor where Dorothy, the Scarecrow, Tin Man, and Cowardly Lion are confronted by the Wicked Witch of the West, flanked by Green-Skinned Guards. Dramatic music swells as the witch cackles menacingly, conjuring a fiery spell. Dorothy urgently cries, "Jerry, quick! The water!" just before the witch shouts, "Don't throw that water!" The spell backfires with a powerful magical explosion and a burst of green smoke, engulfing the witch. She screams, "You cursed brat! Look what you've done! I'm melting, melting! What a world! Who could have thought a good little girl like you would destroy my beautiful wickedness! Look out! I'm going!" Her voice distorts and fades into a gurgling sound as she vanishes in a puff of smoke and ash. The Green-Skinned Guards react with shock, and the group looks on in disbelief, the witch’s broomstick lying discarded on the floor. The New Mouse Character, now standing beside Dorothy and clutching a green bottle, and Jerry the brown mouse, who makes quick squeaking sounds as he scurries nearby, observe the stunned group and frozen guards. One of the Green-Skinned Guards declares in a deep voice, "She's dead. You killed her." Dorothy, her voice filled with distress, replies, "I didn't mean to kill her. Really, I didn't." The atmosphere remains tense and magical, with torches casting flickering light on the stone walls.

event_blocks.summary: In a dimly lit stone corridor, Dorothy, the Scarecrow, the Tin Man, and the Cowardly Lion faced Green-Skinned Guards until Tom and Jerry dramatically crashed into the scene, startling the guards. The setting then transitioned to a grand green hall where a chorus chanted, "Hail to Dorothy! The Wicked Witch is dead!" Tom and Jerry reappeared, with Jerry holding a green bottle, prompting an exclamation of "The broomstick!" A magical whoosh and sparkle followed, and Dorothy inquired, "May we have it?" A deep male voice granted permission, stating, "You freed us all from the Wicked Witch." The Scarecrow then expressed a desire to return to his former profession as a haberdasher. The Wicked Witch of the West suddenly reappeared, demanding, "Why have you come back?" and conjured a fiery spell, causing a green smoke explosion before vanishing, leaving her broomstick and a smoke trail forming the word 'JERRY'. Dorothy pleaded, "Please, sir, we've done what you told us. We've brought you the broomstick of the Wicked Witch of the West," with a New Mouse Character adding, "Dorothy melted her." The deep male voice acknowledged, "You liquidated her, eh?" but then dismissively told Dorothy, "Not so fast. I'll have to give the matter a little thought. Go away and come back tomorrow." The scene concluded with Dorothy and her companions, along with Tom and Jerry, standing in the green hall, the guards looking stunned.

New Mouse Character.description: A small mouse wearing a purple dress with a pink horn, clinging to the Blue Cat’s back during the rope swing, then standing beside Dorothy holding a green bottle after the witch’s defeat. A female voice, possibly hers, confirms that Dorothy melted the witch.

Cowardly Lion.description: A large, brown lion with a green collar, running alongside Dorothy and the Tin Man, looking frightened as the witch’s magic intensifies and then appearing shocked after the witch vanishes.

Broomstick.description: A wooden broomstick with a straw bristle end, held by the witch, used to conjure magic, left discarded on the floor after her disappearance. Its presence is highlighted by a male voice and a magical whoosh/sparkle sound.

Green Hall.description: A grand, emerald-green hall with ornate architecture, featuring tall columns, polished floors, and a large green door.

Tom.description: A blue cat with white paws and belly, initially holding a wooden bucket, then dropping it, being attacked by a green crocodile, and finally collapsing dazed after crashing dramatically into the scene with a loud impact.

Wicked Witch of the West.description: A green-skinned witch with a black pointed hat and cloak, cackling menacingly, holding a broomstick, and conjuring fiery and lightning spells before being engulfed in green smoke and vanishing. She speaks with a menacing, echoing female voice, demanding to know why someone has returned.

Scarecrow.description: A straw-filled figure with a brown hat and green shirt, standing beside Dorothy, arms outstretched in surprise as the witch’s magic erupts and then looking shocked after her vanishing; a male voice, possibly his, expresses a desire to return to an old job as a haberdasher.

Green-Skinned Guards.description: Armored soldiers with green skin, red capes, tall black hats, and ornate spears, marching in formation and surrounding Dorothy and her companions. They are part of a chorus of male voices chanting 'Hail to Dorothy! The Wicked Witch is dead!' and one deep male voice, likely a leader, grants permission for the broomstick, questions Dorothy's actions, and later dismissively delays fulfilling promises. They react with shock as the witch vanishes and appear stunned at the end.

Dorothy.description: A young girl with brown hair tied with blue bows, wearing a blue dress and white apron, standing with her companions, looking alarmed during the witch’s spell and then stunned after her disappearance. She speaks with a clear female voice, first exclaiming about someone being 'on fire,' then asking a hopeful question, pleading her case, and reminding someone of promises.

Stone Corridor.description: A dimly lit, stone-walled passage with torches, where the group is confronted by the witch and her guards, and where the witch conjures her spells, now filled with smoke and ash after her defeat. The initial crash sound occurs here.

Tin Man.description: A metallic figure with a silver body and green vest, holding a small axe, running in alarm as the witch’s spell engulfs the group and then standing stunned after the witch’s defeat.

Smoke Trail.description: A dark, smoky trail left by the witch’s broomstick, forming the words 'SURRENDER DOROTHY' and then 'JERRY' in the sky, causing alarm among the characters.

Jerry.description: A small brown mouse with large ears, scurrying near Dorothy and the New Mouse Character after the witch’s vanishing, appearing alert and cautious. He crashes dramatically into the scene clinging to Tom's back, and is later seen holding a green bottle.

raw_segments: The segment opens in a dimly lit stone corridor where Dorothy, the Scarecrow, the Tin Man, and the Cowardly Lion are surrounded by Green-Skinned Guards. A female voice, likely Dorothy, exclaims, "It's just that he was on fire!" just before Tom the blue cat and Jerry the brown mouse crash dramatically into the scene with a loud impact, disrupting the tense standoff. Tom tumbles dazed, while Jerry clings to his back, causing the guards to react with shock. The scene then shifts to a grand green hall where Dorothy and her companions stand looking alarmed, as a chorus of male voices chants triumphantly, "Hail to Dorothy! The Wicked Witch is dead! Hail! Hail to Dorothy! The Wicked Witch is dead!" accompanied by celebratory music. Tom and Jerry reappear, with Jerry holding a green bottle and Tom looking bewildered. A male voice exclaims, "The broomstick!" followed by a distinct magical whoosh and sparkle sound. Dorothy, with a hopeful female voice, asks, "May we have it?" to which a deep male voice responds, "Of course you can. You freed us all from the Wicked Witch." As the background music continues, a male voice, possibly the Scarecrow, remarks, "Now I can go back to my old job as a haberdasher." The celebratory chanting of "Hail to Dorothy! Hail! Hail to Dorothy! The Wicked Witch is dead!" resumes. Suddenly, the Wicked Witch of the West appears, her menacing, echoing female voice demanding, "Why have you come back?" as she conjures a fiery spell, causing a green smoke explosion that engulfs the area. The witch vanishes in a puff of green smoke, leaving behind her broomstick and a smoke trail forming the word 'JERRY'. A female voice, likely Dorothy, pleads, "Please, sir, we've done what you told us. We've brought you the broomstick of the Wicked Witch of the West." Another female voice, possibly the New Mouse Character, adds, "Dorothy melted her." The deep male voice from earlier responds, "You liquidated her, eh?" Dorothy's female voice then states, "But you see, sir, we'd like you to fulfill your promises to us." The deep male voice, now with a dismissive tone, declares, "Not so fast. Not so fast. I'll have to give the matter a little thought. Go away and come back tomorrow." The scene ends with the group standing in the green hall, now free from the witch’s threat, as Tom and Jerry stand beside them, Jerry holding the green bottle, and the guards looking stunned.

event_blocks.summary: In a dimly lit, green-hued room, Tom the blue cat awakens with a startled scream after Jerry the brown mouse playfully prods him, then flees as Tom gives chase, only to be startled by a black cat. Dorothy, the Scarecrow, the Tin Man, and the Cowardly Lion enter, led by the black cat, to confront the Wizard at a control panel. Dorothy demands to go home, challenging the Wizard's power, who initially warns them in a booming voice to return tomorrow. The Tin Man and Scarecrow react with surprise as the Wizard's voice shifts to a panicked tone, urging them to ignore the "man behind the curtain." The Wizard confesses he is a humbug, admitting he cannot fulfill his promises of a heart, courage, and a brain. Jerry, in a purple dress, reminds them the Wizard promised to send Dorothy home. The Wizard explains he must take Dorothy himself and will fulfill their promises first. Tuffy also requests his promise be kept. Tom appears with Jerry in his mouth, causing shock. Upbeat music begins as the Wizard, holding a cane, leads the group toward a hot air balloon labeled 'STATE FAIR OMAHA'. As the balloon ascends above green-clad citizens, the Wizard declares the Scarecrow will rule in his absence. The Scarecrow, delighted, begins reciting mathematical formulas. The Wizard, with his companions, prepares for a hazardous journey into the stratosphere, assisted by the Tin Man, Lion, and Tuffy, who exclaims his height. The Wizard commands obedience to his appointed rulers. Below, green-uniformed citizens cheer. The Blue Cat, dazed, holds a yellow chick, while the New Mouse Character clutches a green bottle. Dorothy calls out for Toto and pleads with the Wizard not to go. The Wizard, agitated, calls the procedure irregular and unprecedented. As the balloon rises, Dorothy cries out for them to come back, but the Wizard, panicked, states he cannot and doesn't know how it works, bidding them goodbye as the music swells.

Black Cat.description: A small black cat, initially held protectively by Dorothy, later seen in the balloon basket, appearing calm and curious. A distinct 'meow' sound is heard.

Cowardly Lion.description: A large, brown lion with a green collar, standing beside Dorothy, looking frightened and alert. He speaks with a gruff voice, interjecting, 'Ain't it the truth?'

Wizard.description: An elderly man with white hair, wearing a green vest and black coat, initially standing at a control panel, then holding a cane and leading the group to a hot air balloon. His voice is booming and theatrical, exclaiming 'Oh, joy! Rapture!' and making grand pronouncements like 'An isosceles triangle is equal to the square root of the remaining side,' 'Assisted by the Tin Man, by virtue of his heart, the Lion, by virtue of his courage,' and 'And Tuffy, by virtue of his enormous height.' He also commands, 'Obey them as you would me.' Later, his voice becomes agitated, stating, 'Oh, but this is a highly irregular procedure!' and 'This is absolutely unprecedented!' and finally panicked, 'I can't come back! I don't know how it works! Goodbye!'

Green Hall.description: A grand, emerald-green hall with ornate architecture, featuring tall columns, polished floors, and a large green door, housing the control panel and machinery.

Jerry.description: A small brown mouse with large ears, initially in a purple dress with a pink horn, pointing dramatically and speaking with a high-pitched, urgent voice, reminding everyone of a promise, later held by Tom, then seen in the balloon basket, looking up at the Wizard with curiosity.

Scarecrow.description: A straw-filled figure with a brown hat and green shirt, standing beside Dorothy, wiping his eye with a smile as the balloon ascends.

Control Panel.description: A green panel with dials, gauges, and glowing lights, where the Wizard stands, indicating a high-tech, magical environment.

Dorothy.description: A young girl with brown hair tied with blue bows, wearing a blue dress and white apron, holding her black cat protectively and looking concerned. Her voice is heard pleading, 'Please don't go! I'll be right back!' and exclaiming, 'Oh, Toto!' She later urges, 'Boys, please! We don't want to keep the Wizard waiting!' and then cries out in distress, 'Oh, oh my! Come back! Come back! Don't go without me! Please come back!'

Tin Man.description: A metallic figure with a silver body and green vest, holding a small axe, standing beside Dorothy, appearing stunned and observant.

Tom.description: A blue cat with white paws and belly, initially holding Jerry in his mouth, then seen in the balloon basket, looking dazed and surprised.

Green-Clothed Citizens.description: A group of people dressed in green uniforms and dresses, standing in formation outside the hall, waving and cheering as the balloon passes, their cheers contributing to the background sound.

Hot Air Balloon.description: A large balloon with a brown and purple design, labeled 'STATE FAIR OMAHA' in bold letters, carrying the group above a crowd of green-clad citizens.

New Mouse Character.description: A small mouse wearing a purple dress with a pink horn, clinging to the Blue Cat’s back during the rope swing, then standing beside Dorothy holding a green bottle after the witch’s defeat. She speaks with a high-pitched voice, proudly exclaiming, 'I'm huge!'

Blue Cat.description: A blue cat with white paws and belly, initially swinging on a rope with a bucket, then collapsing dazed and soaked after being violently pulled by the electrified rope. Seen here looking dazed and surprised while holding a small yellow chick, accompanied by meowing sounds.

Green Bottle.description: A small green bottle held by the New Mouse Character, which she uses to shrink Tom and later clutches after escaping the hourglass, now visible in her hand after the witch’s vanishing.

Yellow Chick.description: A small yellow chick with blue eyes, seen inside the crate playing with an egg, later watching the chaos with amusement.

raw_segments: The segment opens in a dimly lit, green-hued room with reflective floors and ornate, curved architecture, where Tom, a blue cat, lies dazed and unconscious. Jerry, a brown mouse with a mischievous grin, approaches, playfully pulls a tuft of Tom’s fur, then uses a broomstick to push Tom’s body, causing him to roll and wake up with a loud, startled scream/roar. Jerry scurries away, and Tom chases him, only to be startled by a small black cat emerging from behind a green curtain. The scene shifts as Dorothy, the Scarecrow, the Tin Man, and the Cowardly Lion enter, led by the small black cat. They approach a control panel with glowing dials where the Wizard, an elderly man with white hair in a green vest and black coat, stands. Dorothy, holding the black cat, confronts the Wizard, demanding in her clear, determined voice, "What? But I want to go home now!" and challenging his authority, "If you were really great and powerful, you'd keep your promises!" The Wizard, initially speaking with a deep, booming, echoing voice, warns, "Do not arouse the wrath of the great and powerful Oz. I said come back tomorrow!" and questions, "Do you presume to criticize the great Oz, you ungrateful creatures?" as the group looks on. Suddenly, the Tin Man exclaims with a distinct male voice, "What? Hey, the... stop the... stop there!" and the Scarecrow adds, "Whoa, whoa, whoa!" as the Wizard's voice abruptly shifts from booming to a more normal, slightly panicked tone, urging, "Pay no attention to that man behind the curtain!" He attempts to regain his powerful persona, declaring, "The great Oz has spoken!" but Dorothy directly asks, "Who are you?" The Wizard, now speaking in a resigned, normal voice, confesses, "I? Oh, I am the great and powerful... Wizard of Oz." The Scarecrow then accuses him, "You humbug!" to which the Wizard admits, "Yeah. I'm afraid you're right. I am a humbug." The Tin Man follows up, asking about his promised heart, and the Cowardly Lion questions the courage he was promised, all against the backdrop of the green hall’s machinery and glowing dials, creating a tense yet hopeful atmosphere with ambient machinery sounds.

The segment begins with a close-up of a small mouse, Jerry, in a purple dress with a pink horn, pointing dramatically against a green background, expressing alarm as she urgently reminds everyone in a high-pitched voice, "Don't forget the most important thing. He promised he'd get Dorothy back home to Kansas." The scene shifts to a grand green hall where the Wizard, an elderly man with white hair in a green vest and black coat, stands at a control panel with dials and gauges, addressing Dorothy, the Scarecrow, the Tin Man, and the Cowardly Lion. Dorothy, in her blue dress, holds her black cat protectively, listening intently with the others, who all exclaim "Yeah!" in agreement. The Wizard gestures confidently, explaining in a booming voice, "My diminutive friend, you force me into a cataclysmic decision. The only way to get Dorothy back home to Kansas is for me to take her there myself." The camera zooms in on Dorothy’s concerned face as she asks in a slightly higher tone, "Oh, will you? Could you?" The Wizard smiles and raises his hand, declaring, "I will indeed! But first, I'll fulfill my promises to each of you." A distinct voice, Tuffy, then asks, "Tuffy too? He was going to ask you to make him big," to which the Wizard confirms, "Yes, Tuffy too." Suddenly, Tom the blue cat appears, holding Jerry the brown mouse in his mouth, causing the group to react with a shocked "Oh!" Upbeat, adventurous orchestral music begins with a triumphant flourish as the Wizard, now holding a cane, leads the group toward a large hot air balloon labeled 'STATE FAIR OMAHA'. The balloon ascends above a crowd of green-clad citizens. Inside the basket, the Wizard, Dorothy, the Scarecrow, the Tin Man, the Cowardly Lion, and the black cat are visible. The Wizard declares, "My friends, I, your Wizard, am about to embark upon a hazardous journey into the outer stratosphere!" The crowd below cheers loudly and waves in admiration. The Wizard waves back, then points upward with a mischievous grin, proclaiming, "And I hereby decree that in my absence, the Scarecrow shall rule by virtue of his highly superior brain!" The Scarecrow, clearly delighted, wipes his eye with a smile. The scene ends with the group in the balloon, rising above the green cityscape, as the Wizard continues to speak with confidence, and the Scarecrow, now empowered, begins to recite in a slightly nasal, intellectual tone, "Some of the square roots of any two sides..."

The scene opens with a concerned Scarecrow, the Wizard, dressed in a black coat and green vest, holding a cane, and the anticipatory Tin Man and Cowardly Lion looking up, all set against a backdrop of whimsical, slightly dramatic orchestral music. The Wizard's booming voice declares, "An isosceles triangle is equal to the square root of the remaining side," before exclaiming, "Oh, joy! Rapture!" as he gestures with his cane. The camera pans to reveal Dorothy, holding her black cat, standing beside the Lion. The Wizard then raises his cane, pointing upward, and his voice continues, "Assisted by the Tin Man, by virtue of his heart, the Lion, by virtue of his courage..." The Cowardly Lion interjects with a gruff, "Ain't it the truth?" as the camera follows the cane's tip to reveal a hot air balloon with a woven basket. The Wizard climbs into the basket, followed by Dorothy, the Scarecrow, the Tin Man, and the Cowardly Lion, who are assisted by green-clad attendants. The Wizard continues, "And Tuffy, by virtue of his enormous height," to which the New Mouse Character, in a purple dress with a pink horn, proudly exclaims, "I'm huge!" As the balloon begins to ascend, the Wizard's voice commands, "Obey them as you would me." The camera pulls back to show a grand green hall bustling with cheering, green-uniformed citizens waving below. The Wizard waves back from the basket, while the whimsical music continues, punctuated by a "boing," a "thud," and a distinct "meow." The scene shifts to a close-up of the Blue Cat, looking dazed and surprised while holding a small yellow chick, accompanied by more meowing and barking sounds. The New Mouse Character stands beside Dorothy, clutching a green bottle. The Wizard continues to wave from the ascending balloon, and Dorothy's high-pitched voice exclaims, "Oh, Toto!" As the crowd below cheers and waves, Dorothy pleads, "Please don't go! I'll be right back!" The Wizard's voice, now sounding agitated, interjects, "Oh, but this is a highly irregular procedure!" followed by, "This is absolutely unprecedented!" In a wide shot, the balloon floats higher above the green hall, with the Wizard and his companions looking down at the cheering crowd. Dorothy cries out in distress, "Boys, please! We don't want to keep the Wizard waiting!" then, "Oh, oh my! Come back! Come back! Don't go without me! Please come back!" The Wizard's voice, now panicked, responds, "I can't come back! I don't know how it works! Goodbye!" as the music swells to a dramatic close.

event_blocks.summary: In a green-hued corridor, Dorothy, holding her black cat, expresses distress about returning to Kansas, prompting comfort from the Tin Man and a distressed sound from the Scarecrow, while a Blue Cat and Brown Mouse briefly appear. Their attention is drawn upward to a hot air balloon labeled 'STATE FAIR OMAHA' amidst distant crowd cheering and goodbyes. The Cowardly Lion points to a grand green hall filled with green-clad citizens, where magical twinkling and whooshing sounds accompany a pink light from a crystal ball, revealing the Good Witch of the North. She descends, greeting the group, and in response to Dorothy's plea to go home, transforms the black cat into a small, glowing creature, eliciting delight. The Witch then informs Dorothy that she always had the power to return to Kansas, explaining that the ruby slippers would take her home instantly. With a wave of her wand, the Blue Cat is transformed into a smaller, docile, glowing version beside a proud Jerry, leading Dorothy to exclaim, "Tom and Jerry? And Toto too?" The Witch confirms, "Tom and Jerry, and Toto too," prompting a surprised "Oh!" from the Cowardly Lion. As the Witch instructs Dorothy to tap her heels three times and think, "There's no place like home," the scene focuses on the transformed, content Tom and Jerry, with Jerry holding a small green bottle, and the group smiling.

Green-Clothed Citizens.description: A group of people dressed in green uniforms and dresses, standing in formation outside the hall, waving and cheering 'Goodbye, listen! Goodbye!' as the balloon passes.

Black Cat.description: A small black cat, initially held protectively by Dorothy, later seen transformed into a glowing, magical creature by the Good Witch.

Brown Mouse.description: A small brown mouse with large ears, initially scurrying away, later standing beside the transformed Blue Cat, holding a small green bottle.

Good Witch of the North.description: A woman with long red hair, wearing a pink gown and a tall, pointed hat, holding a wand, who magically transforms the black cat and the Blue Cat, and speaks clearly and reassuringly to Dorothy, explaining her inherent power to return to Kansas via her ruby slippers, and giving instructions to tap her heels and think, 'There's no place like home.'

Cowardly Lion.description: A large, brown lion with a green collar, standing beside Dorothy, looking frightened and alert, exclaiming, 'Look, here's someone who can help you!' upon seeing the Witch, and later making a surprised 'Oh!' sound.

Hot Air Balloon.description: A large balloon with a brown and purple design, labeled 'STATE FAIR OMAHA' in bold letters, carrying a small figure above the green hall.

Green Hall.description: A grand, emerald-green hall with ornate architecture, featuring tall columns, polished floors, and a large green door, housing the control panel and machinery.

Blue Cat.description: A blue cat with white paws and belly, initially dazed and standing beside Jerry, later magically shrunk and glowing with sparkles after the Good Witch’s spell.

WASH & BRUSH UP CO. Sign.description: A large, ornate sign above the entrance of the green hall, displaying the text 'WASH & BRUSH UP CO.' in bold, white letters with a green background.

Scarecrow.description: A straw-filled figure with a brown hat and green shirt, standing beside Dorothy, wiping his eye with a sound of distress as the balloon ascends.

Red Ruby Slippers.description: Dorothy’s iconic red shoes, visible on her feet, standing near the group’s feet at the start of the segment.

Dorothy.description: A young girl with brown hair tied with blue bows, wearing a blue dress and white apron, initially crying and sobbing, expressing her despair about not getting home to Kansas, later asking the Good Witch if she can be sent home, and then surprised to learn she has the power to return. She also asks about Tom, Jerry, and Toto, holding her black cat protectively, which is later transformed into a glowing, magical creature by the Good Witch.

Crystal Ball.description: A glowing purple orb on a golden stand with intricate carvings, reflecting images of the young man and then the Wicked Witch, serving as a magical portal.

Tin Man.description: A metallic figure with a silver body and green vest, holding a small axe, standing beside Dorothy, appearing stunned and observant, and comforting Dorothy by saying, 'Stay with us. We don't want you to go.'

raw_segments: The scene opens with Dorothy, the Scarecrow, the Tin Man, and the Cowardly Lion in a green-hued corridor, looking concerned. Dorothy, holding her black cat, sobs, crying, "Now I'll never get home to Kansas!" The Tin Man comforts her, saying, "Stay with us. We don't want you to go." The Scarecrow wipes his eye with a sound of distress. Briefly, a dazed Blue Cat (Tom) and scurrying Brown Mouse (Jerry) appear. The group's attention shifts upward as a hot air balloon labeled 'STATE FAIR OMAHA' floats overhead, accompanied by distant crowd cheering and shouts of "Goodbye, listen! Goodbye!" The Cowardly Lion exclaims, "Look, here's someone who can help you!" as the camera pans to a grand green hall where green-clad citizens gather. Magical twinkling and whooshing sounds accompany a pink light emerging from a crystal ball, revealing the Good Witch of the North in a pink gown. She descends gracefully, greeting Dorothy and her companions with a warm smile, while gentle, magical music plays. Dorothy, still distressed, asks, "Oh, will you send me home? Can you send me home?" The Witch, using her wand, magically transforms the black cat into a small, glowing creature, which Dorothy holds with delight. The group reacts with joy and relief. The Witch then turns to Tom and Jerry, explaining to Dorothy, "You don't need to be helped any longer. You've always had the power to go back to Kansas." Dorothy, surprised, asks, "I have?" The Witch continues, "Now those ruby slippers will take you home in two seconds." With a wave of her wand, Tom is magically transformed into a smaller, docile, glowing version, standing beside a proud Jerry. Dorothy, noticing them, asks, "Tom and Jerry? And Toto too?" The Witch confirms, "Tom and Jerry, and Toto too." The Cowardly Lion lets out a surprised "Oh!" The scene ends with the group smiling as the Witch places a hand on Dorothy’s shoulder, instructing her, "Just tap your heels three times. And think to yourself, 'There's no place like home.' There's no place like home," as the camera focuses on the transformed, content Tom and Jerry, with Jerry holding a small green bottle.

event_blocks.summary: A young girl with brown hair tied with blue bows, wearing a blue dress and white apron, initially smiles against a green backdrop as a chorus sings "There's no place like home." The music shifts to urgent, distorted orchestral sounds, transitioning from a stormy sky with a 3D animated house to a dark room, and finally a cozy bedroom. In the bedroom, Tom, a blue cat in a green cap, sleeps in a wicker bed labeled 'TOM' with Jerry, a brown mouse. Pizzicato strings and cartoon sounds accompany Jerry as he mischievously removes Tom's cap, waking the cat and initiating a chase around the bed and onto a pink bedspread. The music turns dramatic as the scene shifts to a different room where the young girl, holding a black cat, sits with Aunt Em, an elderly woman with gray hair in a bun, while three men stand nearby. Aunt Em reassures the girl, "You just had a bad dream," and an elderly man asks if she remembers them. One man identifies himself as "your old pal Tubby," and another as "the local Haberdasher." A brief country tune with banjo and fiddle shows a white mouse in overalls and a cowboy hat holding a corn cob next to a man in overalls. Returning to dramatic music, the worried young girl looks up as Aunt Em speaks, and the elderly man expresses relief, "Well, she's all right now, thank heaven." The young girl earnestly responds, "Oh, but I wasn't all right, Aunt Em. We were in terrible danger. But we kept asking people to send us home." In the bedroom, the young woman, now smiling, states, "And they sent us home," prompting a deep laugh from the elderly man. When she asks, "Doesn't anyone believe me?" a black cat meows. Light footsteps and cartoon sound effects signal a chase as Tom, initially dazed and wet, stands by Jerry, who holds a white pitcher. Fast-paced cartoon music plays as Tom, in a green jacket and brown hat, chases Jerry, who wears a chef's hat and apron, taunting him with "Ha ha ha!" Frustrated, Tom grabs a broom and swings it, but Jerry dodges, causing Tom to slip and fall with a splash, spilling the pitcher's contents. Soaked and dejected, Tom watches Jerry place the pitcher on his head and walk away. Tom then smashes the pitcher against the door with a loud crash, shattering it, and Jerry runs away. The scene returns to the bedroom where the group watches with amusement. The young woman, smiling, looks toward the door, her expression shifting to delight, and says, "Anyway, it's all right, because I'm home again. And this is my room. And I love you all." She concludes, "And oh, Annie, there's no place like home." The final shot shows Jerry, wearing a cowboy hat, running across the floor, while Tom, still dazed, sits with the broom stuck to his back amidst fading cartoon music.

raw_segments: The minute opens with a close-up of a joyfully smiling young girl with brown hair tied with blue bows, wearing a blue dress and white apron, set against a green-hued backdrop with uniformed figures, as a chorus of voices gently sings "There's no place like home." This whimsical orchestral music becomes more urgent and slightly distorted as the scene abruptly shifts to a 3D animated house floating in a stormy sky, then a dark, empty room, before transitioning to a cozy bedroom. Here, Tom, a blue cat, sleeps peacefully in a wicker bed labeled 'TOM' with a green cap, alongside Jerry, a brown mouse. Playful pizzicato strings and cartoon sounds accompany Jerry as he mischievously removes Tom’s cap, startling the cat awake and initiating a lively chase around the bed, intensifying as Jerry climbs onto a pink bedspread with Tom leaping after him. The music then turns dramatic and slightly ominous as the scene cuts to a different room where the young girl, now looking concerned and holding a small black cat, sits beside an elderly woman, Aunt Em, who has gray hair in a bun and wears a purple dress. Three smiling men stand nearby. Aunt Em, with a calm, reassuring voice, tells the girl, "You just had a bad dream." One of the elderly men, with a friendly, slightly gruff voice, adds, "Sure you did. You remember us, don't you?" Another man, with a cheerful, higher-pitched voice, refers to himself as "your old pal Tubby," followed by a deeper, slightly formal voice from another man identifying himself as "the local Haberdasher." The scene briefly shifts to a cheerful, country-style tune with banjo and fiddle, showing a white mouse in overalls and a cowboy hat holding a corn cob next to a man in overalls, before returning to the dramatic music and the worried young girl. She looks up as Aunt Em continues speaking, and the elderly man expresses relief, "Well, she's all right now, thank heaven." The young girl, with a distressed and earnest tone, responds, "Oh, but I wasn't all right, Aunt Em. We were in terrible danger. But we kept asking people to send us home."

The scene opens in a cozy, warmly lit bedroom with floral wallpaper and wooden floors, where an elderly woman, an elderly man, a young woman, and two younger men gather around a bed. The young woman, dressed in a blue dress with a white apron and her brown hair tied with blue bows, speaks with a gentle voice, stating, "And they sent us home." The elderly man responds with a deep laugh. The young woman then expresses concern, asking, "Doesn't anyone believe me?" A small black cat, sitting beside her, lets out a distinct meow. The focus then shifts to a different part of the house, where light footsteps and cartoon sound effects signal the start of a chase. Tom the blue cat, initially dazed and wet, stands beside Jerry the brown mouse, who holds a white pitcher. Whimsical, fast-paced cartoon music begins as Tom, now wearing a green jacket and a brown hat, playfully chases Jerry. Jerry, who dons a white chef’s hat with a red tomato emblem and a small apron, dodges and taunts Tom with a distinctive "Ha ha ha!" laugh. Tom, frustrated, grabs a broom and swings it, but Jerry dodges, causing Tom to slip and fall with a splash, spilling water from the pitcher. Soaked and dripping, Tom looks dejected as Jerry stands triumphantly. Jerry then places the pitcher on his head and walks away. In a fit of rage, Tom grabs the pitcher and smashes it against the door with a loud crash, shattering it, letting out distressed "Oh!" sounds. Jerry, startled, runs away as Tom, holding the broken pitcher, looks utterly defeated. The scene returns to the bedroom, where the group watches the chaos unfold with amusement. The young woman, now smiling, looks toward the door, her expression shifting from concern to delight. She speaks again, her voice filled with relief, "Anyway, it's all right, because I'm home again. And this is my room. And I love you all." She concludes, addressing someone, "And oh, Annie, there's no place like home." The final shot shows Jerry, now wearing a small cowboy hat, running across the wooden floor, while Tom, still dazed, sits on the floor with the broom stuck to his back, looking utterly defeated amidst the fading cartoon music.

event_blocks.summary: The segment concludes with end credits scrolling vertically against a dramatic, stormy sky backdrop of swirling dark blue and gray clouds. White text with black shadows lists various production roles, including Sound Designer SCOTT COCHRAN, Audio Engineering MICHAEL COVURN, and Orchestrations by MICHAEL TAVERA. Simultaneously, a lively musical medley from 'The Wizard of Oz' plays, featuring distinct character voices singing "If I only had a brain," variations of the Cowardly Lion's song, and lines like "I'd be brave as a blizzard." The medley transitions into an ensemble rendition of "We're off to see the wizard, the wonderful wizard of Oz!" as the credits continue to scroll, highlighting featured songs and performers. The credits proceed to list Production Administrator NICOLE MARTIN, Production Support staff, Casting and Accounting personnel, Executive in Charge of Music ATHENA HURROATE, Business & Legal Affairs staff, and Production Management BOB B. RASE. The final frames display copyright information for 'Tom and Jerry & The Wizard of Oz' © 2011 Turner Entertainment Co., the bold title 'TOM AND JERRY, THE WIZARD OF OZ,' a legal disclaimer, and the Warner Bros. Entertainment Company logo and website, www.warnerbros.com, all centered against the same dramatic stormy sky backdrop, as the orchestral music fades out, leaving a brief silence.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a serene rural landscape at dusk, featuring a dirt path, wooden fences, a barn, and a wagon under a stormy sky, all set to a grand, sweeping orchestral score that evokes a sense of adventure and resolution. As the scene transitions to a dark, cloudy sky, the elegant white cursive text "The End" appears, followed by the bold blue and white "A Tom and Jerry CARTOON" logo, with the cinematic orchestral music continuing to swell. The background remains a swirling, moody sky as white credit text with black shadows begins to roll, listing voice actors like Grey DeLisle for Dorothy, Joe Alaskey for the Wizard of Oz, and Laraine Newman for the Wicked Witch of the West, clearly indicating a Wizard of Oz-inspired narrative. Around the 0:36 mark, the orchestral music gently shifts, and a male voice begins to sing the iconic song "If I Only Had a Brain," accompanied by a whimsical orchestral backing. The song continues as production credits, including Assistant Production Managers, Animation Intern, Droopy Sequence Director, Storyboard artists, Animation Timing, and Character Layout and Animation, scroll by, with the dramatic stormy sky and the familiar melody consistently emphasizing the grandeur and fantasy elements of the story's conclusion.

The minute opens with end credits scrolling vertically against a dynamic, stormy sky backdrop filled with dark, swirling clouds in shades of deep blue and gray, with occasional breaks of lighter blue and white. White text with a black drop shadow lists various production roles and names, including Character Layout and Animation, Animation Cleanup, Character Design, Background Design, Background Paint, Ink & Paint, Effects Animation, Animation Services, Supervising Director, Producer, Director, and Key Animation. Simultaneously, a male baritone voice sings a whimsical yet melancholic orchestral song, strongly reminiscent of the Scarecrow's "If I Only Had a Brain" from The Wizard of Oz. The lyrics express a desire for intelligence, with lines like "With the thoughts you'd be thinking you could be another Lincoln if you only had a brain" and "I could tell you why the ocean's near the shore." The song continues, with the singer lamenting being "just a nothing" and having a "head awful full of stuffin'," wishing for a brain to dance and be merry. Around the 39-second mark, the singer exclaims a brief "Woo!" before the music transitions slightly, becoming more reflective. The song then shifts to lyrics about being an "empty kettle" and "torn apart," still yearning for a brain or a more complete existence, maintaining a self-pitying yet hopeful tone. The dramatic and cinematic orchestral music perfectly underscores the turbulent visual atmosphere of the stormy sky and the continuous upward scroll of the credits, creating an integrated sense of closure and reflection for the preceding cartoon.

The minute features end credits scrolling vertically against a dramatic, stormy sky backdrop, characterized by swirling dark blue and gray clouds with occasional lighter patches. White text with a black shadow lists various production roles, including Layout, Key Animation, Model Checking, Inbetweeners, Backgrounds, and Coloring Lead. Simultaneously, a male vocalist performs a classic, theatrical song with full orchestral backing, singing lyrics like "If I only had a heart, I'd be tender, I'd be gentle, and awful sentimental regarding love and art." The song continues, with a brief, higher-pitched female voice interjecting "Romeo" during a lyrical reference to a balcony scene, as the credits progress to list Ink and Paint, Scan, Camera, Production, Recording Facilities, and Post-Production roles. The vocalist expresses a desire to "register emotion, jealousy, devotion, and really feel the part," and to "stay young and chipper" if he only had a heart. The scrolling credits culminate with the song 'OVER THE RAINBOW' by Harold Arlen and E.Y. Harburg, performed by Nikki Yanofsky, which perfectly complements the ongoing musical performance. The male vocalist's song then fades out with "Yeah, it's sad, believe me, Missy, when you're born...". The dynamic stormy sky remains a consistent, immersive backdrop throughout the entire credit sequence.
